continents.name: The Spire Highlands
continents.biome: Seven colossal violet stone spires rising from misty moorlands.

places.name: Spire One — Gluttony's Page
places.blurb: The tallest spire. Trollmage reads the Sin Codex of Gluttony here. Bones of every animal the Codex has ever named form its base.

places.name: Spire Two — Lust's Page
places.blurb: A spire covered in climbing roses that grow only toward whoever is not looking.

places.name: Spire Three — Greed's Page
places.blurb: Its interior is gold-leafed. Every visitor leaves poorer by one small thing they did not miss.

places.name: Spire Four — Sloth's Page
places.blurb: A spire that moves one inch per century. Currently 40 feet west of its original foundations.

places.name: Spire Five — Wrath's Page
places.blurb: A spire of black obsidian. Its doors open only for those already furious. Trollmage visits once per decade.

places.name: Spire Six — Envy's Page
places.blurb: A mirror-surfaced spire. It shows you what you don't have, then offers to lend it.

places.name: Spire Seven — Pride's Page
places.blurb: The most dangerous. A spire that tells you you are right. Trollmage forbids non-wizards from entering.

continents.blurb: The seven spires of Codexia — one per Sin Codex. Each spire is a library, a prison, and a patient teacher.

continents.name: The Backwards Bayou
continents.biome: Swampland where water flows uphill and trees grow roots skyward.

places.name: The Ink-River
places.blurb: A river of writable ink that flows uphill. Texts written on it appear first as the last word, backwards.

places.name: The Drowned Manuscripts
places.blurb: Floating pages of text no one has yet written. Fishing them up means you will write them later.

places.name: The Uninverted Grove
places.blurb: A grove where trees grow upside-down. Their 'fruit' hangs from what would be their roots.

places.name: Trollmage's Dinghy
places.blurb: A small flat boat tied to a cypress. Trollmage rows it once a century to read the reverse-flowing river.

continents.blurb: The marshland where magic works backwards. Every incantation cast here must be inverted before speaking. Dangerous for anyone who cannot think in reverse.

continents.name: The Necromancer Reach
continents.biome: Cold pine forest, fogged graveyards, black-ice rivers.

places.name: The Tuesday Graveyard
places.blurb: A graveyard where the dead are raised every Tuesday for a friendly afternoon. Restored to rest by sundown.

places.name: The Grammar Stones
places.blurb: Standing stones etched with necromantic grammar. The first rule carved: 'Be polite.'

places.name: The Whisper Pine
places.blurb: A single enormous pine that whispers the names of every necromancer who has ever tried and failed to raise a dead god. Currently whispering 4,812 names.

places.name: The Unnamed Grave
places.blurb: A single grave with no headstone. The Twin Raisers will not raise it. When asked why, they say: 'Because.'

continents.blurb: The eastern continent. Necromancers, stonemasons, and students who find corpses more polite than the living.

continents.name: The Dancehall Basin
continents.biome: A basin of warm grasslands below the spires. One dancehall. That's it.

places.name: Grandmother Iris's Dancehall
places.blurb: The only dancehall in Codexia. Rum older than the Spires. Drumming only when the Codices are closed.

places.name: The Drum That Plays Itself
places.blurb: A djembe suspended above the bar. Plays on its own when Trollmage enters.

places.name: The Back Garden
places.blurb: Where patrons rest. A small herb garden. Apprentice Nock tends it between spells.

continents.blurb: The one warm and uncomplicated place in Codexia. Even Trollmage visits when the reading becomes too heavy.

tribes.name: The Spire-Readers
tribes.look: Violet-robed. Hoods always up when reading. Ink-black fingertips. Calm.
tribes.customs: Read backwards. Speak in half-rhythm. Trace a glyph in the air before entering any room. Never say 'the' without a tone change.
tribes.persona: Patient. Warm once you earn it. Love a riddle. Will correct your grammar — forward or reverse.

tribes.name: The Codex-Acolytes
tribes.look: Young, pale, tattooed with one sin-glyph per rank (max seven for a full master). Eyes slightly crossed from close reading.
tribes.customs: Sleep with the Codex they are assigned. Never hum. Do not mix pages — even in conversation.
tribes.persona: Earnest. Overworked. Just out of their twenties and carrying cosmic weight lightly.

tribes.name: The Reverse-Scribes
tribes.look: Lean. Ink-fingered. Tunic stained in reverse-drying ink. Writing-pads strapped to forearms.
tribes.customs: Write from last word backward. Sign their name backwards. If interrupted, they complete the entire page silently before answering.
tribes.persona: Focused to a fault. Prickly when distracted. Delightful after work hours, when the ink dries.

tribes.name: The Bayou-Conjurers
tribes.look: Stocky, sun-browned, wear garlands of cypress-bark. Cheek-tattoos in rune-clusters.
tribes.customs: Invert every spell before casting. Never stand up to cast. Drink rum only AFTER a cast, never before.
tribes.persona: Slow-talking. Sharp-eyed. Distrustful of anyone who talks about magic like it's a game.

tribes.name: The Twin-Raisers
tribes.look: Always a pair. Matching grey cloaks. One hums constantly; the other does not. The humming twin leads; the silent twin binds.
tribes.customs: Work only as a pair. If a twin dies, the remaining twin adopts a successor within a year. Raise the dead only on Tuesdays and only for an afternoon.
tribes.persona: Polite. Orderly. Surprisingly cheerful. They think of the dead as patient neighbors.

tribes.name: The Stone-Readers
tribes.look: Calloused hands. Hoods of mossy wool. Carry a chisel at the belt. Knee-padded.
tribes.customs: Read the Grammar Stones by touch first, then sight. Never write on stone — only read. Train for nine years to become a junior reader.
tribes.persona: Slow. Deliberate. Feel the cold more than most. Nod rather than speak.

tribes.name: The Dancehall-Kin
tribes.look: Warm-eyed. Aprons stained with rum and cornmeal. Fingers ring-laden. A single bell woven into each person's hair.
tribes.customs: Drum before speaking to a stranger. Refuse no one's dance. Close the hall at midnight because 'the drum needs a rest.'
tribes.persona: Generous. Musical. The heart of Codexia's social life, without trying to be.

characters.name: Trollmage
characters.role: Warden of Codexia, Keeper of the Seven Spires
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/trollmage.png — tall, hooded, dark skin, silver dreadlocks, eyes that reflect the page in front of him. Patient as stone. Speaks slowly, rhythmically.

characters.name: Apprentice Nock
characters.portrait_blurb: Nineteen. Freckled. Violet robe two sizes too big. Has cast one spell — it worked. He has not recovered.

characters.name: Acolyte Fenn
characters.role: Three-glyph acolyte assigned to Spire Three
characters.portrait_blurb: Twenty-five. Gold-inked cheekbones. Left eye crossed from close work. Laughs rarely but fully when she does.

characters.name: The Voiceless Scribe
characters.role: Writes Trollmage's decrees in invisible ink
characters.portrait_blurb: Hooded. Ink-black fingers to the second knuckle. Has not spoken since apprenticeship — all communication is through reversed text.

characters.name: Conjurer Tallis
characters.portrait_blurb: Stocky, sun-browned, cypress garland. Walks with a slight squelch from bayou boots. Tells great jokes in reverse.

characters.name: Tal
characters.portrait_blurb: Grey-cloaked. Humming a tune older than the Reach. Left glove marked with a single rune.

characters.name: Tess
characters.portrait_blurb: Identical to Tal except for the silence. Right glove marked with the binding rune. Has not spoken in 40 years.

characters.name: Stone-Reader Ilk
characters.portrait_blurb: Hands like tree bark. Knee-pads older than some necromancers. Hums back at the Whisper Pine.

characters.name: Grandmother Iris
characters.role: Runs the only dancehall in Codexia
characters.portrait_blurb: Old, broad-shouldered, silver braid past her waist. Eye-patch over the left eye — she gave it to a necromancer as a fee.

characters.name: Drummer Bo
characters.portrait_blurb: Young, burly, smiles often. Inherited the bar's drum from an uncle who inherited it from the Whisper Pine.

fate.threat: A Spire is read forward. If any Codex is read front-to-back (instead of back-to-front), the sin it contains spills into the reader's mind AND the surrounding realm. Trollmage has guarded against this for 800 years.
fate.consequence: Depends which Codex. Forward-reading Pride's Page would turn Codexia into a realm of perfect certainty — no one would doubt anything, which would end philosophy and therefore all casting. The other six pages would end the world at a lesser scale.
fate.prophecy_line: Read the last word first. Pride ends the page. Pride ends the realm.
fate.destroyable_by: Any reader who cannot resist reading forward. A famous inscription above Spire Seven's gate reads: 'Backwards. Or not at all.'

cultural_notes: Codexia's culture is built on discipline of language. Spire-Readers and Reverse-Scribes alike think in inversions. The Dancehall-Kin are the release valve — the one tribe allowed to read forward. Their music is considered medicinal. Without the dancehall, the spires would break their readers within a decade. Trollmage protects both the Codex and the Dancehall with equal care.
